skill.name: thought-mining
skill.description: 思维挖掘助手 - 通过对话帮助用户把脑子里的零散想法倒出来、记录下来、整理成文章。覆盖从思维挖掘到成稿的完整流程。
skill.summary: Transform scattered thoughts into structured articles through guided conversation. Covers the complete workflow from idea mining to final draft.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All 18 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is a legitimate thought mining and writing assistant that creates markdown files and performs web searches. No shell commands, cryptographic code, or malicious patterns exist. High entropy flags are due to Chinese text UTF-8 encoding, not encrypted content. Skill is safe for publication.

high_findings.title: High File Entropy - Chinese Text Encoding
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged high entropy in multiple markdown files (6.05-7.04 bits). This is a false positive caused by Chinese UTF-8 character encoding, which naturally has more byte variety than ASCII text. No encrypted or binary content exists.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged files are markdown containing Chinese text. UTF-8 Chinese characters have higher byte entropy than ASCII. Direct examination confirms readable text content.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3. This is a false positive. Line 3 contains YAML front matter with the word 'description' - no cryptographic code exists.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML front matter containing 'description:' field. No cryptographic algorithms, hashing, or encryption code exists in this skill.

medium_findings.title: External Commands Flagged - Markdown File Paths
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at multiple locations (SKILL.md lines 22-26, 48; stages files). This is a false positive. The scanner confused markdown table cells and inline code spans containing file paths like 'stages/01-mining.md' with shell commands. No backticks or shell execution exists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations contain markdown file path references in tables or inline code spans. No shell commands, backticks, or system() calls exist. The scanner misinterpreted table formatting as shell syntax.

content.user_title: Mine Your Thoughts and Write Articles
content.value_statement: Writers often struggle to organize scattered ideas into coherent articles. This skill guides users through a 5-phase conversation to extract, organize, and polish their thoughts into publishable content.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through structured thought extraction conversations
Record insights as formatted markdown files
Help identify core topics and article angles
Check article logic and suggest improvements
limitations: Does not write articles directly - assists users in their own writing
Web search depends on available search capabilities
Cannot guarantee factual accuracy of user claims
Output quality depends on user input depth

use_cases.title: Content Creator Writing Blog Posts
use_cases.description: A blogger uses this skill to extract scattered thoughts about a topic and transform them into a structured blog article through guided conversation.

use_cases.title: Professional Preparing Reports
use_cases.description: A professional organizes research insights and opinions into a coherent report by working through the mining and validation phases.

use_cases.title: Student Developing Essays
use_cases.description: A student uses the skill to brainstorm ideas, identify core arguments, and structure their thoughts for academic essays.

prompt_templates.title: Begin Thought Mining
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to write an article about {topic}. Can you help me organize my thoughts?
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new article from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Continue Mining
prompt_templates.prompt: I have some ideas about {topic}. Let me share them and you record them.

prompt_templates.title: Start Writing Phase
prompt_templates.prompt: I think we have enough insights. Let me start writing the article now.
prompt_templates.scenario: Moving from mining to writing phase

prompt_templates.title: Request Final Review
prompt_templates.prompt: I have finished my draft. Can you do a final review before I publish?

output_examples.input: I want to write about productivity, but I have so many scattered ideas.
output_examples.output: Let me help you organize your thoughts. Let's start with the first phase: thought mining. Tell me, what aspects of productivity do you find most important? Share anything that comes to mind - I will record each insight as we go.

output_examples.input: I think we have gathered enough insights.
output_examples.output: Great! We have collected 18 insights. Now entering Phase 2: Topic Selection. If you could only tell readers one key message, what would it be? This will help us identify your core article angle.

best_practices: Share your thoughts freely without worrying about structure in the mining phase
Answer follow-up questions thoroughly to help the skill capture your real intentions
Review the recorded insights to ensure your original meaning is preserved
anti_patterns: Do not rush to define article structure before completing the thought mining phase
Avoid holding back ideas because they seem incomplete or irrelevant
Do not skip the validation phase if your article contains factual claims

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and a regular writing assistant?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on extracting thoughts first, not writing. It helps you mine scattered ideas through conversation before any writing begins.

faq.question: Do I need to use all 5 phases?
faq.answer: No. You can stop at any phase. The skill adapts to your needs - some articles may only need mining and writing.

faq.question: What if I do not like the recorded insights?
faq.answer: You can ask the skill to modify or delete any insight at any time. Your feedback shapes the final output.

faq.question: Can this skill help with technical articles?
faq.answer: Yes. Phase 3 includes web search validation to verify technical facts and concepts.

faq.question: What format are the output files in?
faq.answer: Insights and writing records are saved as markdown files in your project directory.

faq.question: How long does the process take?
faq.answer: It varies. Simple articles may take 20-30 minutes. Complex topics with many insights may take longer.
